known_projects.project: Cohort context intelligence layer running in TEE; this codebase.
Confidential compute network providing CVM (TEE) execution.
Reproducible TEE image building, packaged as a Make-OSI-based SDK.
Tool for building custom Debian spin-off ISOs; used by EZTE.
Reproducible TEE infrastructure project from Flashbots.
Articulation practice app with speech evaluation.
Biotech data-sharing platform for research collaboration.
Agent operating system based on a Debian fork.

known_technologies.tech: C++ runtime for running LLM models locally

example.chunk: [Hang Yin] when I was working on the service mesh, there is decision I need to make. So, how should we define the policy? One way is that we verify the exact code running in each CVM, so eventually it become we define a few compose hash, and we have a white list of the compose hash, then if the compose hashes match, we allow them to join this service mesh. And another way is we can verify the app ID, because the app ID is the unit you define the workload in DStack. But app ID, I didn't go with app ID, because that will make the deployment a little bit harder. I have to define the app ID first, then define the policy and wire everything together. With compose hash, I can pre calculate all the measurements before the deployment, and then just deploy it once.
example.expected: { "summary": "Hang Yin chose compose-hash-based policy over app-ID-based policy for the DStack service mesh; compose-hash lets measurements be pre-calculated and the deployment ships in one step instead of needing to wire app ID, policy, and code together.", "signals": [ {"kind": "decision", "text": "Use compose-hash whitelisting (not app-ID whitelisting) for the DStack service mesh policy", "source_quote": "I didn't go with app ID, because that will make the deployment a little bit harder", "said_by": ["Hang Yin"], "about_person": []}, {"kind": "insight", "text": "Compose-hash whitelisting lets all measurements be pre-calculated before deployment, so the policy ships in a single deploy step", "source_quote": "I can pre calculate all the measurements before the deployment, and then just deploy it once", "said_by": ["Hang Yin"], "about_person": []} ], "entities": [ {"name": "DStack", "type": "project", "evidence": "the service mesh he's defining policy for"}, {"name": "service mesh", "type": "concept", "evidence": "the system whose policy is being defined"}, {"name": "compose hash", "type": "technology", "evidence": "the chosen whitelisting key"}, {"name": "app ID", "type": "technology", "evidence": "the rejected alternative whitelisting key"}, {"name": "CVM", "type": "technology", "evidence": "the unit each compose hash measures"}, {"name": "KMS", "type": "technology", "evidence": "guarantee app ID would have provided automatically"} ], "topics": ["attestation", "service mesh", "deployment"] }

example.chunk: [Alex (flashbots?)] I will send you the link to the specific project. It's github.com/easy te [Shaw] easy te easy hyphen te, [Alex (flashbots?)] yeah, if you're forking tails, this will make your life a lot easier, because I consider forking tails, and I went down that rabbit hole for like three months
example.expected: { "summary": "Alex (Flashbots) offered to share the EZTE GitHub link with Shaw, after Shaw mentioned he is forking Tails for his agent OS work.", "signals": [ {"kind": "action_item", "text": "Alex will send Shaw the github.com/easy-te link", "source_quote": "I will send you the link to the specific project. It's github.com/easy te", "said_by": ["Alex (flashbots?)"], "about_person": ["Shaw"]}, {"kind": "insight", "text": "Tails forking is a 3-month rabbit hole; EZTE compresses that to a simpler workflow", "source_quote": "I went down that rabbit hole for like three months", "said_by": ["Alex (flashbots?)"], "about_person": []} ], "entities": [ {"name": "EZTE", "type": "project", "evidence": "github.com/easy-te shared as the project link"}, {"name": "Tails", "type": "technology", "evidence": "the Debian-based live OS Shaw is forking"}, {"name": "Alex (flashbots?)", "type": "person", "evidence": "speaker offering the link", "affiliation": "flashbots"}, {"name": "Shaw", "type": "person", "evidence": "recipient of the link, forking Tails"} ], "topics": ["reproducible builds", "agent operating systems"] }

example.chunk: [Andrew Miller] Does anyone here done anything interesting with cross-chain bridges? How about sending any transactions from a tee? Oh, yeah, Demers has a hy Tina [Andrew Miller] Tina. Also, I think is in New York already, just quite here in person.
example.expected: { "summary": "Andrew Miller asked the room for experience with cross-chain bridges and TEE-originated transactions; question left open within this chunk. He also noted Tina is in New York but not yet physically present.", "signals": [ {"kind": "open_question", "text": "Has anyone done interesting work with cross-chain bridges or sending transactions from a TEE?", "source_quote": "Does anyone here done anything interesting with cross-chain bridges? How about sending any transactions from a tee?", "said_by": ["Andrew Miller"], "about_person": []}, {"kind": "impactful_point", "text": "Tina is in New York already but not yet physically present at the meeting", "source_quote": "Tina. Also, I think is in New York already, just quite here in person", "said_by": ["Andrew Miller"], "about_person": ["Tina"]} ], "entities": [ {"name": "cross-chain bridges", "type": "technology", "evidence": "subject of the open question"}, {"name": "TEE", "type": "technology", "evidence": "subject of the open question"}, {"name": "Andrew Miller", "type": "person", "evidence": "asked the open question"}, {"name": "Tina", "type": "person", "evidence": "mentioned as in New York but not present at the meeting"} ], "topics": ["cross-chain", "confidential compute"] }

example.chunk: [Albiona Hoti] The actual user journey is basically get on the app, you select the different themes of topics that you want to talk about, and the depth, and then you read for one to two minutes, and you record yourself on the app speaking about it. The app used to give you only an overview of basically the awards, closet structure, pace and time, and the delivery on organization, security, language, all of these little details, but then now added another session where we evaluate also the content of what we're speaking, because people were saying like saying a bunch of other things the content itself, and I didn't check that in the beginning, and now it also checks the content itself. For now I have 524 users, out of which our users are hundreds of things, or I think people who have done more density sessions.
example.expected: { "summary": "Albiona shipped a content-quality evaluation pass on Elocute on top of the existing delivery-quality metrics (pace, structure, organization, language); the app now scores both how you say it AND what you said. Currently has 524 users.", "signals": [ {"kind": "decision", "text": "Added a content-evaluation pass to Elocute on top of the existing delivery-evaluation metrics", "source_quote": "now added another session where we evaluate also the content of what we're speaking", "said_by": ["Albiona Hoti"], "about_person": []}, {"kind": "insight", "text": "Users were unhappy with delivery-only scoring because they could game it by talking a lot without saying much", "source_quote": "people were saying like saying a bunch of other things the content itself", "said_by": ["Albiona Hoti"], "about_person": []}, {"kind": "impactful_point", "text": "Elocute has reached 524 users, with a denser power-user segment doing repeated sessions", "source_quote": "For now I have 524 users", "said_by": ["Albiona Hoti"], "about_person": []} ], "entities": [ {"name": "Elocute", "type": "project", "evidence": "the app being presented"}, {"name": "Albiona Hoti", "type": "person", "evidence": "the presenter"}, {"name": "articulation", "type": "concept", "evidence": "the core skill the app trains"} ], "topics": ["product evolution", "articulation", "speaking practice"] }

example.chunk: [Andrew Miller] The last problem that this solves is just like I now appreciate the importance of human water coolers, or people who act as health connections. My exemplar for this is Tita, who you'll get to meet later tonight or tomorrow. She's been my entrepreneurship mentor since I dropped out of university to work on TV-based with Chevy, and the mode at which she works — she's like your most effective human router that anyone who knows her has seen. This is someone who makes it their job to pick up context about everyone that they can in their orbit, often through just like very effective prompting.
example.expected: { "summary": "Andrew framed the Router project around the 'human water cooler' role — people who absorb and route context across a community — using Tita, his entrepreneurship mentor, as the canonical exemplar.", "signals": [ {"kind": "insight", "text": "The 'human water cooler' archetype — someone who absorbs and effectively routes context across a community — is what the Router project is trying to instrument with software", "source_quote": "I now appreciate the importance of human water coolers, or people who act as health connections", "said_by": ["Andrew Miller"], "about_person": []}, {"kind": "impactful_point", "text": "Tita is the canonical example of a high-effectiveness human router; she will be at the cohort tonight or tomorrow", "source_quote": "My exemplar for this is Tita, who you'll get to meet later tonight or tomorrow", "said_by": ["Andrew Miller"], "about_person": ["Tita"]} ], "entities": [ {"name": "Router", "type": "project", "evidence": "the project being motivated"}, {"name": "Tita", "type": "person", "evidence": "named exemplar of a human-router role; Andrew's entrepreneurship mentor"}, {"name": "Andrew Miller", "type": "person", "evidence": "the speaker/presenter"}, {"name": "human water cooler", "type": "concept", "evidence": "Andrew's framing for the archetype the Router project targets"} ], "topics": ["context routing", "community building"] }

example.chunk: [Andrew Miller] Do you have on the demo that you said, do you have, like, an up and running service version of this? Like, is this something that runs on, like, an Ethereum testnet or something? [Chloe Wang] No, it's currently not deployed, it's only running local.

style_guide.kind: A course of action the group agreed on. Past or present tense ("we decided", "let's go with", "we should").
A concrete next step someone agreed to do. Often "I'll send", "you handle", "can you", "let me reach out".
A question raised in this chunk that is NOT answered within the same chunk.
A non-obvious observation or learning. Use sparingly — prefer a more specific kind when one fits.
A consequential statement that doesn't fit decision/action/question but matters for prep. Use rarely.

team_context.open_world_note: The lists above are NON-EXHAUSTIVE. New projects, technologies, people (including guests joining a single meeting), and topics WILL appear in transcripts and must be extracted faithfully even when not listed here. Treat the lists as ANCHORS for known entities, not as a closed vocabulary.
